nouns.summary: The speaker says these things so that the listeners may “have peace” in him (ἐν ἐμοὶ εἰρήνην ἔχητε), presenting peace as something they are to possess in their relationship with him.
The speaker states that, in the world, the listeners “have tribulation” (ἐν τῷ κόσμῳ θλῖψιν ἔχετε), describing their ongoing experience of trouble while they are in the world.

The speaker mentions the world as the sphere where the listeners experience tribulation, and also declares that he “has conquered the world” (ἐγὼ νενίκηκα τὸν κόσμον), encouraging them to take courage based on his victory over it.
